sourceMovie.title: Clue
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Clue for the same reason it broke through in 1985: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Jonathan Lynn keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Murder by Death
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Clue clicked for you, Murder by Death (1976) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and thriller, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Clue clicked for you, Knives Out (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Clue?
faq.answer: Try Murder by Death, Knives Out, Game Night—each shares DNA with Clue while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Clue?
faq.answer: Clue (1985) is best described as Comedy, Thriller, Crime, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Clue worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
